text.title: Backup Strategy: 3-2-1-1-0 and Tool Selection
text.summary: The 3-2-1-1-0 rule, immutable storage implementation, RPO/RTO tiers, and tool selection by backup target.

rule.statement: Implement the 3-2-1-1-0 rule: 3 copies, 2 media types, 1 offsite, 1 immutable, 0 verified recovery errors.
rule.rationale: Traditional 3-2-1 does not protect against ransomware because all three copies can be on network-reachable storage. The immutable copy cannot be encrypted or deleted even by admin credentials. The zero-error component mandates automated restore verification — backups that have never been tested are not backups.

example.note: Example 3-2-1-1-0 layout for a production PostgreSQL database: Copy 1 = local NAS (disk). Copy 2 = AWS S3 Standard-IA (cloud). Copy 3 = S3 bucket with Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode, 90-day retention (immutable). Zero errors = automated pg_restore to isolated test DB nightly.

rule.statement: Use COMPLIANCE mode (not GOVERNANCE) for S3 Object Lock when regulatory requirements apply — COMPLIANCE cannot be overridden even by the root account.
rule.rationale: GOVERNANCE mode allows users with s3:BypassGovernanceRetention permission to delete objects. COMPLIANCE mode is truly immutable for the retention period.

list.item: AWS S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE — cannot be deleted by any IAM principal during retention
Azure Immutable Blob Storage WORM policy — time-based or legal hold
GCP Bucket Lock retention policy — bucket-level immutability
Physical WORM tape — highest security, slowest recovery

rule.statement: Define RPO and RTO per data class before selecting backup frequency — the numbers drive tool choice, not the reverse.
rule.rationale: A team that chooses daily backups and then discovers their RPO is 1 hour must redesign the entire backup system. Start with business requirements.

list.item: Critical (RPO < 1h, RTO < 1h): continuous replication or WAL streaming + frequent base backups
Standard (RPO < 24h, RTO < 24h): daily full + transaction logs
Archive (RPO < 1 week, RTO < 1 week): weekly full to cold storage
PostgreSQL: pg_basebackup + WAL archiving for PITR; pg_dump for logical backups
MySQL/MariaDB: XtraBackup for hot physical backups; mysqldump for logical
MongoDB: mongodump --oplog for PITR on replica sets
File systems: Restic (deduplication + encryption, multi-backend) or BorgBackup (space-efficient archives)
Kubernetes: Velero (cluster-level, PVC snapshots) or Kasten K10 (enterprise app-aware)
Cloud-native: AWS Backup, Azure Backup, GCP Cloud Backup for managed service integration
